summary: Bryan Cantrill writes a memorial tribute to Khaled Bichara, an angel investor in Oxide Computer who died in a car accident in Cairo. He remembers Khaled as both a bold investor with appreciation for hard technical problems and a profoundly decent person dedicated to his family, companies, and country. The post reflects on Khaled's work fostering entrepreneurship in Egypt and expresses the team's grief at losing someone they had hoped to journey with for years.
key_points: Khaled Bichara was an Oxide angel investor who died in a car accident in Cairo
He was known for appreciating hard technical problems while being a deeply caring person
His work kindling entrepreneurship in Egypt inspired many
Oxide was looking forward to a long partnership with him
money_quotes: Those of us who have known Khaled for years have known him to be a bold investor who appreciated hard technical problems â and also a profoundly decent person, who cared deeply for his family, his companies, and his country.
Khaledâs stories of kindling entrepreneurship in Egypt were inspiring to any who heard them â and served as a reminder of the reponsibility that we all have to our broader communities.
For our part, we were looking forward to a long journey together, and to making good on his belief in us; we are gutted to have lost him so abruptly.
Silicon Valley may be a long way from Cairo, but his impact and legacy will be felt here for years to come.
key_insight: The best investors combine bold technical vision with profound human decency, and their impact extends far beyond the companies they fund to the communities and entrepreneurs they inspire.
